title: The Frontier of Health
body:
The frontier now includes AI diagnostics, telehealth, wearables, and precision medicine. Health data is becoming richer and more continuous, which can improve detection and monitoring.

Genomics and longevity research are pushing the field toward more personalised prevention and treatment. At the same time, the rise of chronic disease means systems must become better at long-term management, not only acute interventions.

Mental health access and digital care are also expanding quickly. The big question is how to make these tools trustworthy, equitable, and clinically useful rather than merely fashionable.

Health is becoming more data-driven without becoming less human.
keyPoints: Longevity and prevention are getting more attention
Mental health access remains a major system challenge
